title: Hikaru Utada
wikitext:
[[Image:Hikaru Utada.jpg|right|Hikaru Utada]]
'''Hikaru Utada''' (宇多田光) is one of Japan's most popular singers and was born in New York City on January 19, 1983. The child of a producer (father) and musician (mother), they attended high school at the American School in Tokyo and later dropped out of Columbia University where they majored in singing in order to pursue their musical career. While they were in Tokyo, they recorded for Toshiba-EMI, releasing many singles, most notably ''First Love'', which was also released in an album of the same title. In 2007, they released [[Beautiful World]] which was used in the [[Rebuild of Evangelion]] [[Evangelion 1.0: You Are (Not) Alone]] movie and many of its trailers. Utada's [[Fly Me to the Moon]](in other words), which was released on the same single as Beautiful World, was also used in one of the trailers. They sang the theme songs [[Sakura Nagashi]] and [[One Last Kiss]] for [[Evangelion 3.0]] and [[Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0]], respectively. 

During a June 26 social media livestream with [[Hideaki Anno]], Utada [https://www.instagram.com/p/CQQil30pg34/?utm_source=ig_embed&ig_rid=859d15cf-7059-404c-8092-f923a2bb7d9b came out as non-binary]. '''This article has been revised to reflect this by referring to Utada in gender-neutral terms.'''

==Notes==
*Utada was married to Kazuaki Kiriya, a photographer and director who had directed many of their music videos. They were married in September 7, 2002 and divorced in March 2, 2007.
*Hikaru Utada also goes by ''Hikki'' and ''Cubic U'', the latter a name they for their U.S. debut album.
*Utada has won several Nihon Golden Disk awards for "Song of the Year" and "Pop/Rock Album of the Year".
*The debut of ''Beautiful World / Kiss & Cry'' on August 27, 2007 made its way to #3 on Japan’s Oricon Weekly Singles Charts. However, on September 1, 2007, the day the first Rebuild of Evangelion movie opened in theaters, it shot up to #1.
*Utada was #24 in an HMV Japan survey of the Top 100 J-pop artists of all time.

html:
Hikaru Utada (宇多田光) is one of Japan's most popular singers and was born in New York City on January 19, 1983. The child of a producer (father) and musician (mother), they attended high school at the American School in Tokyo and later dropped out of Columbia University where they majored in singing in order to pursue their musical career. While they were in Tokyo, they recorded for Toshiba-EMI, releasing many singles, most notably First Love, which was also released in an album of the same title. In 2007, they released Beautiful World which was used in the Rebuild of Evangelion Evangelion 1.0: You Are (Not) Alone movie and many of its trailers. Utada's Fly Me to the Moon(in other words), which was released on the same single as Beautiful World, was also used in one of the trailers. They sang the theme songs Sakura Nagashi and One Last Kiss for Evangelion 3.0 and Evangelion: 3.0 + 1.0, respectively.

During a June 26 social media livestream with Hideaki Anno, Utada came out as non-binary. This article has been revised to reflect this by referring to Utada in gender-neutral terms.

Notes[edit]

  • Utada was married to Kazuaki Kiriya, a photographer and director who had directed many of their music videos. They were married in September 7, 2002 and divorced in March 2, 2007.
  • Hikaru Utada also goes by Hikki and Cubic U, the latter a name they for their U.S. debut album.
  • Utada has won several Nihon Golden Disk awards for "Song of the Year" and "Pop/Rock Album of the Year".
  • The debut of Beautiful World / Kiss & Cry on August 27, 2007 made its way to #3 on Japan’s Oricon Weekly Singles Charts. However, on September 1, 2007, the day the first Rebuild of Evangelion movie opened in theaters, it shot up to #1.
  • Utada was #24 in an HMV Japan survey of the Top 100 J-pop artists of all time.
